articles.title: Smokers in China are being chided by anti-smoking women
articles.summary: Many many many men smoke in China. A small but outspoken group of women are becoming anti-smoking advocates, confronting those who light up in public.

articles.title: Abortion pills "just in case"? Planned Parenthood will offer them in two states
articles.summary: In Washington state and Hawaii, residents can now get mifepristone and misoprostol from Planned Parenthood to keep in their cabinets in case they need to end a pregnancy at a later time.

articles.title: Federal Panel Approves Trump’s Plans for a 250-foot Arch in Washington
articles.summary: The panel, whose members were selected by President Trump, has an advisory role on the design of the project but no enforcement power.

articles.title: Forecasters expect slightly fewer hurricanes than usual this year, but the risk of destructive storm
articles.summary: Forecasters expect 8 to 14 storms will form in the Atlantic between June 1 and November 30. But the danger is more serious than the numbers suggest.

articles.title: GOP races to fund immigration enforcement. And, U.S. indicts former Cuban president
articles.summary: Republicans are racing to pass a $72 billion immigration enforcement package. And, the U.S. has indicted former Cuban President Raúl Castro for his alleged role in the 1996 downing of two planes.

articles.title: The U.S. threatens to revoke the Palestinian U.N. ambassador's visa
articles.summary: A leaked State Department memo shows the U.S. threatened to revoke Palestinian visas if they pushed for a senior United Nations post.

articles.title: After the sirens: Lebanon's first responders swing between duty and grief
articles.summary: Nearly 3,000 people have been killed and nearly 1 million have been displaced the war in southern Lebanon began in March. Nearly 400 have been killed since a ceasefire began in April.

articles.title: Trump’s $1.8 Billion Fund in the Cross Hairs
articles.summary: Critics say the deal creates a “slush fund,” a term with a colorful maritime history.

articles.title: A trillion dollar question: Will SpaceX's Starship launch go well?
articles.summary: Ahead of a much anticipated IPO, SpaceX is carrying out a critical test of its giant, stainless steel rocket. Investors will be watching closely.

articles.title: Walmart plans price cuts using tariff refunds as shoppers get skittish
articles.summary: Now that the U.S. government must refund most tariffs, Walmart says it might put its refund money toward lowering store prices. Executives say the cost of gas has shoppers increasingly under stress.

articles.title: Supreme Court Permits Lawsuits Over U.S. Assets Seized by Cuba in 1960
articles.summary: The Trump administration backed lawsuits brought by the Havana Docks Corporation that would allow the U.S.-owned entity to get compensation for property confiscated by Fidel Castro’s regime.

articles.title: In Blackout-Hit Cuba, Word of U.S. Castro Indictment Spreads Slowly
articles.summary: While many Cubans were divided over the legitimacy of the U.S. charging Raúl Castro with murder, the hope for developments that might ease their suffering is widespread.

articles.title: Trump’s Fund Shows Blanche Choosing Loyalty Over Pushing Back
articles.summary: Once seen by some as the most conventional of President Trump’s political appointees, Todd Blanche has taken off the gloves in his new role as acting attorney general.

articles.title: Susan Collins brings federal dollars to Maine. She's hoping that's worth it to voters
articles.summary: As Maine's Senate matchup is all but set, incumbent Republican Sen. Susan Collins urges voters to pick her over Democrat Graham Platner because she can fund state priorities due to her seniority.

articles.title: Stephen Colbert’s Final ‘Late Show’ Marks the End of an Era
articles.summary: Stephen Colbert’s “Colbert Report” satirized politics. Then his “Late Show” confronted a moment when politics became self-satirizing.

articles.title: Experimental Drug Yields Dramatic Weight Loss
articles.summary: People who got the injection, retatrutide, lost 28 percent of their body weight on average after 80 weeks, Eli Lilly said.

articles.title: Democrats’ Midterm Strength Masks Fierce Divides and Frustration, Poll Shows
articles.summary: Democratic voters are in a combative, anti-establishment mood, unhappy with their party and disagreeing about its best path forward, a New York Times/Siena poll found.

articles.title: As floods get worse, Britain tries a new solution: beavers
articles.summary: About 400 years ago, beavers were hunted to extinction across Britain. Now they're being reintroduced as little climate warriors, as communities harness their dam-building skills to mitigate flooding.

articles.title: Netanyahu scolds Israeli security minister for videos taunting flotilla activists
articles.summary: Israel's national security minister triggered a backlash after releasing videos taunting detained flotilla activists who tried to breach the blockade of Gaza, telling them they should be imprisoned.

articles.title: Ebola fears surge on the ground in Congo over rapid spread of a rare type
articles.summary: Healthcare workers in eastern Congo said Wednesday they are underprotected and undertrained in a rapidly spreading Ebola outbreak of a rare type of the virus in one of the world's most vulnerable plac

articles.title: Liberal U.S. mayors team up with European counterparts to fight authoritarianism
articles.summary: Ten U.S. mayors from cities such as Chicago and Cincinnati have joined a pact with European mayors to defend democracy and progressive values and fight right-wing populists and authoritarianism.

articles.title: Cuba's Raúl Castro indicted over 1996 fatal downing of two aircraft
articles.summary: Miami prosecutors have prepared an indictment against former Cuban president Raúl Castro over the 1996 downing of two NGO planes that killed four people onboard.

articles.title: Dominion still has pending lawsuits against election deniers such as Rudy Giuliani and Sidney Powell
